name: Cauliflower with Beef "Sichuan Style
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Lan chi black bean paste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the steak into 1/2 inch cubes.
2. Make the black bean paste by blending all paste ingredients until smooth.
Combine soy and sherry for marinade. Marinate steak pieces for 1 hour, massaging meat to aid tenderizing. Drain, reserving marinade.
3. Wash and soak mushrooms for 1 hour; drain, reserving soaking liquid, then thinly slice.
4. Wash, peel, and roll-cut carrots (slicing on a slight bias into 1-inch lengths but turning the carrot a quarter turn between slices).
5. Wash cauliflower, trim off thick stems, and cut into pieces about the size of the carrot pieces.
6. Parboil carrots in boiling stock for about 3 minutes or until just beginning to soften. Reserve carrots and stock.
7. Stir-fry:
- Heat peanut oil in a hot wok.
- When oil just begins to smoke, add drained steak cubes; stir-fry briskly for 1 minute until meat begins to lose pinkness. Do not overcook the meat or it will be tough. Remove to a holding plate.
- Swirl remaining oil into the wok.
- Add ginger, garlic, and Szechuan peppercorns; stir-fry for 15 seconds.
- Add cauliflower and mushrooms; stir-fry for 1 minute.
- Add reserved carrot stock, marinade, and mushroom soaking liquid. Bring to a boil.
- Reduce heat to medium, cover wok, and simmer for 3 minutes.
7. Remove lid, turn the heat to high. When sauce boils again, add carrots and beef.
8. Mix together. Splash vinegar down sides of wok.
9. Thicken with thin cornstarch paste. Sauce should be a light gravy.
10. Add sesame oil. Toss in green onion.
11. Serve.
